title: Plasma Ionization Chamber Calibration
description: A lantern serves as a sealed reaction chamber containing ionizable gas mixtures, while a PrimaryIonDrive acts as the electromagnetic excitation source. The user must calibrate ion beam energy levels, adjust gas mixture ratios, and balance charge conservation across multiple ionization stages to achieve a target plasma state, with full undo/redo support for each calibration step.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) implementing a plasma ionization calibration simulation. Load Lantern.glb centered at origin as the 'reaction chamber' and PrimaryIonDrive.glb positioned to its right as the 'ion excitation source'. Use OrbitControls, a PerspectiveCamera at (0,3,6), ambient light (0.3) and a point light inside the lantern that changes color based on plasma state.

Core mechanics: The lantern chamber holds a virtual gas mixture of 3 elements (Hydrogen, Helium, Neon) with adjustable mole fractions (sliders, must sum to 1.0 — conservation of mass). The ion drive has an adjustable beam energy (1-10 eV slider) and beam focus (narrow/medium/wide toggle). A 'Fire Ion Beam' button sends a visible particle stream (animated small spheres) from the drive into the lantern.

Implement 6 ionization stages: each firing ionizes the gas mixture differently based on energy vs ionization potentials (H:13.6eV cumulative threshold, He:24.6eV, Ne:21.6eV). Track total charge state inside the chamber — charge must be conserved (electrons removed = net positive charge displayed). Show a real-time HUD with: current charge balance, ionization percentages per element, temperature estimate (proportional to total energy deposited), and stage count.

The user must reach a target plasma state shown on screen (e.g., 60% H ionized, 40% He ionized, Ne neutral, net charge +4). Changing gas ratios resets ionization but preserves energy history. Implement an undo/redo stack (Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y and buttons) tracking every discrete action (slider change, beam firing, toggle). Minimum 10-14 steps expected: adjust 3 sliders, toggle focus, fire beam multiple times, possibly undo mistakes. Show particle glow effects inside the lantern (PointLight color shifts blue→white→violet with ionization). Display success message with green outline when target state is achieved within ±5% tolerance.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  hydrogenFraction: number,         // mole fraction of hydrogen in gas mixture (0-1)
  heliumFraction: number,         // mole fraction of helium in gas mixture (0-1)
  neonFraction: number,         // mole fraction of neon in gas mixture (0-1)
  beamEnergy: number,         // ion beam energy in eV (1-10)
  beamFocus: string,         // beam focus mode (narrow|medium|wide)
  ionizationStage: number,         // current ionization stage (0-6)
  totalCharge: number,         // net positive charge in chamber (electrons removed)
  ionizationEvents: number,         // cumulative count of ionization events
  chamberPressure: number,         // virtual pressure in chamber (Pa)
  beamActive: boolean,         // whether ion beam is currently firing
  calibrationComplete: boolean,         // whether all 6 stages achieved with charge balance
}
